title: astragalus turolensis subsp. aragonensis
intro: Astragalus turolensis subsp. aragonensis, also known as Astragalus aragonensis, is a plant species belonging to the legume family Fabaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Astragalus aragonensis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. This plant is used in traditional herbal preparations, often in the form of infusions, decoctions, or capsules, to support respiratory health, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of fatigue, stress, and anxiety.
